description: A designed 'self-deportation' promotional graphic in portrait format. The image shows three young people seen from behind (two with backpacks, hair tied up, one with short dark hair) gazing toward a glowing, warm-toned world map / globe rendered in fiery orange and blue tones with sun flare, suggesting travel abroad and a hopeful horizon. A dark gradient overlay at the bottom carries white and light-blue text: 'With self-deportation, you have' in white sans-serif, followed by a large light-blue script/serif word 'choices'. A solid dark-blue footer bar with a subtle pinstripe pattern contains the round DHS seal and the 'ICE' wordmark on the left, a vertical divider, and 'Learn more at: ICE.gov/self-deportation' on the right. The aspirational, upbeat color palette and forward-gazing youths are designed to reframe self-deportation as an empowering personal 'choice' rather than coercion. Propaganda technique: positive/euphemistic reframing and soft persuasion encouraging unauthorized immigrants to leave voluntarily (the 'CBP Home'/self-deport campaign). Target audience: undocumented immigrants, especially younger ones, nudging voluntary departure.
notable_text: With self-deportation, you have choices; Learn more at: ICE.gov/self-deportation
